Problem Statement: True or False. Justify your answer with a proof or a counterexample. Assume all functions $$f$$ and $$g$$ are continuous over their domains.
Question: If $$f ( x ) > 0 , f^{\prime} \text{(} x ) > 0$$ for all $$x ,$$ then the right-hand rule underestimates the integral $$\int_{a}^{b} f ( x ) .$$ Use a graph to justify your answer.

Problem Statement: True or False. Justify your answer with a proof or a counterexample. Assume all functions $$f$$ and $$g$$ are continuous over their domains.
Question: $$\int_{a}^{b} f ( x )^{2} d x = \int_{a}^{b} f ( x ) d x \int_{a}^{b} f ( x ) d x$$

Problem Statement: True or False. Justify your answer with a proof or a counterexample. Assume all functions $$f$$ and $$g$$ are continuous over their domains.
Question: If $$f ( x ) \le g ( x )$$ for all $$x \in [ a , b ] ,$$ then $$\int_{a}^{b} f ( x ) \le \int_{a}^{b} g ( x ) .$$

Problem Statement: True or False. Justify your answer with a proof or a counterexample. Assume all functions $$f$$ and $$g$$ are continuous over their domains.
Question: All continuous functions have an antiderivative.

Problem Statement: Evaluate the Riemann sums $$L_{4} \text{and} R_{4}$$ for the following functions over the specified interval. Compare your answer with the exact answer, when possible, or use a calculator to determine the answer.
Question: $$y = 3 x^{2} - 2 x + 1$$ over $$[ -1 , 1 ]$$

Problem Statement: Evaluate the Riemann sums $$L_{4} \text{and} R_{4}$$ for the following functions over the specified interval. Compare your answer with the exact answer, when possible, or use a calculator to determine the answer.
Question: $$y = \text{ln} ( x^{2} + 1 )$$ over $$[ 0 , e ]$$

Problem Statement: Evaluate the Riemann sums $$L_{4} \text{and} R_{4}$$ for the following functions over the specified interval. Compare your answer with the exact answer, when possible, or use a calculator to determine the answer.
Question: $$y = x^{2} \text{sin} x$$ over $$[ 0 , \pi ]$$

Problem Statement: Evaluate the Riemann sums $$L_{4} \text{and} R_{4}$$ for the following functions over the specified interval. Compare your answer with the exact answer, when possible, or use a calculator to determine the answer.
Question: $$y = \sqrt{x} + \frac{1}{x}$$ over $$[ 1 , 4 ]$$

Problem Statement: Evaluate the following integrals.
Question: $$\int_{-1}^{1} ( x^{3} - 2 x^{2} + 4 x ) d x$$

Problem Statement: Evaluate the following integrals.
Question: $$\int_{0}^{4} \frac{3 t}{\sqrt{1 + 6 t^{2}}} d t$$

Problem Statement: Evaluate the following integrals.
Question: $$\int_{\pi \text{/} 3}^{\pi \text{/} 2} 2 \text{sec} ( 2 \theta ) \text{tan} ( 2 \theta ) d \theta$$

Problem Statement: Evaluate the following integrals.
Question: $$\int_{0}^{\pi \text{/} 4} e^{\text{cos}^{2} x} \text{sin} x \text{cos} x d x$$

Problem Statement: Find the antiderivative.
Question: $$\int \frac{d x}{( x + 4 )^{3}}$$

Problem Statement: Find the antiderivative.
Question: $$\int x \text{ln} ( x^{2} ) d x$$

Problem Statement: Find the antiderivative.
Question: $$\int \frac{4 x^{2}}{\sqrt{1 - x^{6}}} d x$$

Problem Statement: Find the antiderivative.
Question: $$\int \frac{e^{2 x}}{1 + e^{4 x}} d x$$

Problem Statement: Find the derivative.
Question: $$\frac{d}{d t} \int_{0}^{t} \frac{\text{sin} x}{\sqrt{1 + x^{2}}} d x$$

Problem Statement: Find the derivative.
Question: $$\frac{d}{d x} \int_{1}^{x^{3}} \sqrt{4 - t^{2}} d t$$

Problem Statement: Find the derivative.
Question: $$\frac{d}{d x} \int_{1}^{\text{ln} ( x )} ( 4 t + e^{t} ) d t$$

Problem Statement: Find the derivative.
Question: $$\frac{d}{d x} \int_{0}^{\text{cos} x} e^{t^{2}} d t$$

Problem Statement:
The following problems consider the historic average cost per gigabyte of RAM on a computer. \begin{table}[h]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Year & 5-Year Change ($) \\ \hline
1980 & 0 \\ \hline
1985 & -5,468,750 \\ \hline
1990 & -755,495 \\ \hline
1995 & -73,005 \\ \hline
2000 & -29,768 \\ \hline
2005 & -918 \\ \hline
2010 & -177 \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}
Question: If the average cost per gigabyte of RAM in 2010 is $12, find the average cost per gigabyte of RAM in 1980.

Problem Statement:
The following problems consider the historic average cost per gigabyte of RAM on a computer. \begin{table}[h]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Year & 5-Year Change ($) \\ \hline
1980 & 0 \\ \hline
1985 & -5,468,750 \\ \hline

1995 & -73,005 \\ \hline
2000 & -29,768 \\ \hline
2005 & -918 \\ \hline
2010 & -177 \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}
Question: The average cost per gigabyte of RAM can be approximated by the function $$C ( t ) = 8 , 500 , 000 ( 0.65 )^{t} ,$$ where $$t$$ is measured in years since 1980, and $$C$$ is cost in US$. Find the average cost per gigabyte of RAM for 1980 to 2010.

Question: The velocity of a bullet from a rifle can be approximated by $$v ( t ) = 6400 t^{2} - 6505 t + 2686 ,$$ where $$t$$ is seconds after the shot and $$v$$ is the velocity measured in feet per second. This equation only models the velocity for the first half-second after the shot: $$0 \le t \le 0.5 .$$ What is the total distance the bullet travels in 0.5 sec?
